column1: # design decision D2: "keep the shell literals, lint them for equality" — a
# TSV of every existing knob, populated with defaults matching the shell
# literals EXACTLY, so a future equality lint (a LATER item,
# registry-config-lints) starts green with no baseline needed. This file is
# header for the parse/union contract and the overlay extension format.
# name           the shell variable name (exact, case-sensitive).
# default        the CURRENT shell literal, copied verbatim from the
#                interpolation written as-is (never pre-resolved). A blank
#                field means the literal default IS the empty string.
# type           closed set (see below) — the KIND of value, not its
# layer          closed set (see below) — which rung of the six-rung config
#                DEFAULT lives at. Almost every row is `kernel` (an
#                individual script's own hardcoded rung-6 fallback) or
#                that file IS the committed config a consuming repo vendors
#                and edits, per its own header). A var whose default
#                genuinely DIFFERS between two owning scripts (a real rung-5
#                vs rung-6 divergence, not just a byte-identical duplicate
#                fallback — see below) gets TWO rows, one per layer.
# owning-script  repo-relative path to the script whose `:=`/`:-` line this
#                row transcribes. Several knobs have a BYTE-IDENTICAL `:=`
#                fallback duplicated in more than one consuming script
#                (documented in each duplicate site's own comment as
#                also has a matching literal in funnel-drive.sh and
#                funnel-tick.sh); this registry records ONE row citing the
#                script documented as the SOURCE OF TRUTH (build.config.sh
#                where one exists) rather than one row per duplicate site,
#                since the literals are identical and a future equality
#                lint gains nothing from redundant rows.
# doc            one-line description of what the knob controls.
#   string   free-form text with no narrower type below
#   enum     a closed set of string values (see the doc column for the set)
#   path     a filesystem path, or a bare binary name resolved via PATH
#   label    a GitHub label / branch / literal identifier string
#   marker   an HTML-comment idempotency marker embedded in issue/PR bodies
# The six-rung ladder's own tokens (docs/config-precedence.md). Rungs 1-2
# (cli, env) never appear here — a registry row records a DEFAULT, and rungs
# 1-2 are call-site/environment inputs, never a hardcoded default:
#   cli            rung 1 — CLI flag (never a registry row; listed for
#   env            rung 2 — env var (never a registry row; ditto)
#   machine-conf   rung 3 — a machine-wide conf file
#   repo-local     rung 4 — an untracked, gitignored repo-local conf file
#   tracked-repo   rung 5 — a COMMITTED config file's own `:=` default
#                  (build.config.sh today — the file IS the tracked conf)
#   kernel         rung 6 — a hardcoded `:=`/`:-` fallback in an individual
#                  script, for a non-vendoring caller that never sources a
# A row exists for every `${VAR:-default}` / `: "${VAR:=default}"` shell
# seam an OPERATOR could meaningfully override via the config precedence
# ladder to change behavior. Excluded: internal/derived/computed values
# (timestamps, loop counters, computed paths built from other already-
# environment-detection predicates (`${TMUX:-}` — "am I inside tmux?", not a
# OS/XDG passthrough vars with no project-specific override point of their
# explicitly documented as such in their own script (`BUILD_QUOTA_NOW`,
# `FUNNEL_NOW_*`). A "test-double seam for the <X> binary" row (e.g.
# `*_GH_BIN`) IS included — swapping the resolved binary is a real,
# meaningful override, not merely a test's implementation detail.
# Two rows below (FOUNDATION_KERNEL_REPO, KERNEL_REMOTE) record a CURRENT
# default that is this org's real repo clone URL — permanent, load-bearing,
# out of scrub scope (this repo's own public identity, not a personal-token
# leak; see check-personal-token-denylist.sh's category-1 rationale). They
# carry an explicit `denylist:allow` marker substring in their doc column
# check-personal-token-denylist.sh's marker-skip already uses in shell
# files, adapted to TSV's data-not-code rows, since the checker's marker
# test is a bare substring match, not shell-comment syntax) — those rows
# would otherwise register as NEW denylist hits, and the burn-down baseline
# mechanism explicitly does not accept new rows for an already-tracked
# family (see personal-token-denylist-baseline.tsv's own header). These are
# permanent, documented, load-bearing defaults, not scrub debt — exactly the
# A THIRD row used to live here — KNOWLEDGE_STORE_ROOT's build.config.sh
# reseed to the operator's personal vault directory. kernel-literal-scrub
# removed that reseed (build.config.sh no longer sets a rung-5 default for
# this knob at all) rather than allow-marking it, so the row was deleted
# rather than converted — see the kernel-layer KNOWLEDGE_STORE_ROOT row
# (owning-script knowledge_store.sh) below for the knob's one remaining
# TEMPERLOOP_PROFILE (below) is a RESERVED row, not a live seam today: no
# script reads it yet. The profile mechanism itself (named config profiles
# an operator switches between) is deferred (Bucket C of the D2 design); this
# row only reserves the name + default so a future implementation has an
# uncontested slot and the registry's shape doesn't change out from under
# an equality lint when that lands.
# See row count at end of file (grep -c for a live count; comment count
